title: [突發] 科學家證實, 越有錢越開心, 越窮越唔開心

replies.msg:
In US, annual household income < 85k 就會越小錢越唔開心
> 85k 就會越多錢越開心

HK 要幾多錢先開心?




Harvard psychology doctoral student and former software product manager Matthew Killingsworth was developing a measurement tool, an iPhone app called Track Your Happiness that pings users at random intervals and asks about their activities and feelings, often using a sliding scale for answers.
The conclusion, just published in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, is that while the connection is stronger for life satisfaction than it is for experienced well-being, it doesn’t disappear for the latter after $75,000 or $90,000. Money keeps buying happiness, even for the affluent.

https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2021-01-25/can-money-buy-you-happiness-yes-but-there-s-a-catch

真

replies.msg:
BERTRAND RUSSELL & ALFRED NORTH WHITEHEAD – Principia Mathematica 1+1=2

Some idea of the scope and comprehensiveness of the “Principia” can be gleaned from the fact that it takes over 360 pages to prove definitively that 1 + 1 = 2.

Today, it is widely considered to be one of the most important and seminal works in logic since Aristotle’s “Organon”. It seemed remarkably successful and resilient in its ambitious aims, and soon gained world fame for Russell and Whitehead. Indeed, it was only Gödel’s 1931 incompleteness theorem that finally showed that the “Principia” could not be both consistent and complete.

https://www.storyofmathematics.com/20th_russell.html

quote.msg:
屌明明人地唔至咁嘅意思，
佢兩個variable係 evaluation同experienced ,講緊窮嘅會amplify唔開心嘅嘢，有錢嘅會amplify開心嘅嘢，背後個cognitive bias嘅mechanism其實好值得探討，因爲關乎到快樂係點黎。
再者，75000呢個係好值得研究嘅數，點解係75000唔係8萬唔得9萬？
75000就買到positive嘅life evaluation所反映緊嘅係一個地方成個市場經濟物價政策等等好多嘅情況。
如果有兩個地方嘅呢個交叉點唔同，又可以對比到係乜嘢嘅唔同所導致
重有人地係用一個新啲嘅方法去做measurements，推翻咗以前嘅認知。
再加上measure well-being本身唔係一件易嘅事，時地人唔同已經會令每個well-being components嘅weighting唔同，所以你見到佢用好多sub scale再睇例如 angry/stress同income有冇association，從而可以搵出到底income係經咩pathway黎影響percepted well-being。

好多呀媽係女人嘅道理人人都知，但唔係個個知點解，
冇科學研究你點知男女點分？冇social science study你點知點define呀媽？做study唔係一件兒戲嘅事，亦正正係日常encounter緊但未有人知點解係咁，先有研究嘅價值。

BERTRAND RUSSELL & ALFRED NORTH WHITEHEAD – Principia Mathematica 1+1=2

Some idea of the scope and comprehensiveness of the “Principia” can be gleaned from the fact that it takes over 360 pages to prove definitively that 1 + 1 = 2.

Today, it is widely considered to be one of the most important and seminal works in logic since Aristotle’s “Organon”. It seemed remarkably successful and resilient in its ambitious aims, and soon gained world fame for Russell and Whitehead. Indeed, it was only Gödel’s 1931 incompleteness theorem that finally showed that the “Principia” could not be both consistent and complete.

https://www.storyofmathematics.com/20th_russell.html

HK 要幾多錢先開心?




Harvard psychology doctoral student and former software product manager Matthew Killingsworth was developing a measurement tool, an iPhone app called Track Your Happiness that pings users at random intervals and asks about their activities and feelings, often using a sliding scale for answers.
The conclusion, just published in the Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences, is that while the connection is stronger for life satisfaction than it is for experienced well-being, it doesn’t disappear for the latter after $75,000 or $90,000. Money keeps buying happiness, even for the affluent.

https://www.bloomberg.com/news/articles/2021-01-25/can-money-buy-you-happiness-yes-but-there-s-a-catch
